title: Apium tenuifolium
intro: Apium tenuifolium, commonly known as sea celery or rock celery, is a perennial plant that belongs to the parsley family (Apiaceae). It has been used in traditional medicine for its health benefits, which include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and acting as an antimicrobial agent. The bioactive constituents of Apium tenuifolium include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and essential oils, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant are teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support immune function and alleviate gastrointestinal issues.
taxonomy: Apium tenuifolium belongs to the family Apiaceae, commonly known as the carrot or parsley family. This plant is a type of wild carrot native to New Zealand. It is classified as a perennial herb and is also known as sea carrot or New Zealand wild carrot. The species name 'tenuifolium' is derived from the Latin words 'tenuis,' meaning thin or slender, and 'folium,' meaning leaf. The plant's classification also includes the genus Apium, which is a group of umbelliferous plants.
